div.head: The Secretary of State to the Chargé in Nicaragua (Munro)
div.p: 157. It is Cumberland’sDr. W. W. Cumberland, since 1923 Financial Adviser and Customs Receiver for the Haitian Government. plan to resign and undertake financial work in New York, leaving Haiti by December 15. He has informed the Department that he could make a financial survey of Nicaragua in the interim between leaving Haiti and beginning work in New York. It is estimated that the time required for this work is about three months. Both McCoy and DoddsDr. Harold W. Dodds, who accompanied General McCoy on his trip to Nicaragua. Dr. Dodds had been engaged in 1921 by the Nicaraguan Government to assist in the revision of the electoral laws of Nicaragua. See Foreign Relations, 1923, vol. ii, pp. 605 ff.; also ibid., 1924, vol. ii, pp. 487 ff. believe this advisable. Department desires your views. Cable.
